479ŌåÆ// DeferredReason is a string that describes why a resource was deferred.\n   480ŌåÆ// It differs from the protobuf enum in that it adds more cases\n   481ŌåÆ// since it's more widely used to represent the reason for deferral.\n   482ŌåÆ// Reasons like instance count unknown and deferred prereq are not\n   483ŌåÆ// relevant for providers but can occur in general.\n   484ŌåÆtype DeferredReason string\n   485ŌåÆ\n   486ŌåÆconst (\n   487ŌåÆ\t// DeferredReasonInvalid is used when the reason for deferring is\n   488ŌåÆ\t// unknown or irrelevant.\n   489ŌåÆ\tDeferredReasonInvalid unknown.\n   493ŌåÆ\tDeferredReasonInstanceCountUnknown DeferredReason = \"instance_count_unknown\"\n   494ŌåÆ\n   495ŌåÆ\t// DeferredReasonResourceConfigUnknown is used when the reason for deferring\n   496ŌåÆ\t// is that the resource configuration was unknown.\n   497ŌåÆ\tDeferredReasonResourceConfigUnknown DeferredReason = \"resource_config_unknown\"\n   498ŌåÆ\n   499ŌåÆ\t// = \"provider_config_unknown\"\n   502ŌåÆ\n   503ŌåÆ\t// DeferredReasonAbsentPrereq is used when the reason for deferring is that\n   504ŌåÆ\t// a required prerequisite resource was absent.\n   505ŌåÆ\tDeferredReasonAbsentPrereq DeferredReason = \"absent_prereq\"\n   506ŌåÆ\n   507ŌåÆ\t// DeferredReasonDeferredPrereq is used when the reason for deferring is\n   508ŌåÆ\t// that a required prerequisite resource was itself deferred.\n   509ŌåÆ\tDeferredReasonDeferredPrereq DeferredReason = \"deferred_prereq\"\n   510ŌåÆ)\n   511ŌåÆ\n   512ŌåÆtype Deferred struct {\n   513ŌåÆ\tReason If any arguments were unknown then the result may\n   819ŌåÆ\t// optionally be unknown. The type of the returned value must conform\n   820ŌåÆ\t// to the return type constraint for this function as declared in the\n   821ŌåÆ\t// provider schema.\n   822ŌåÆ\t//\n   823ŌåÆ\t// If Diagnostics contains any errors, this field will be ignored and\n   824ŌåÆ\t// so can be left as cty.NilVal to represent the absence of a value.\n   825ŌåÆ\tResult cty.Value\n   826ŌåÆ\n   827ŌåÆ\t// Err is the error value from the function call. Usually you don't need to call this method directly as GetProviderSchema\n\t// will merge the identity schemas into the provider schema.\n\tGetResourceIdentitySchemas() GetResourceIdentitySchemasResponse\n\n\t// ValidateProviderConfig allows the provider to validate the configuration.\n\t// The ValidateProviderConfigResponse.PreparedConfig field is unused. The\n\t// final configuration is not stored in the state, and any modifications\n\t// that need to be made must be made during the Configure method call.\n\tValidateProviderConfig(ValidateProviderConfigRequest) ValidateProviderConfigResponse\n\n\t// ValidateResourceConfig allows the provider to validate the resource\n\t// configuration values.\n\tValidateResourceConfig(ValidateResourceConfigRequest) ValidateResourceConfigResponse\n\n\t// ValidateDataResourceConfig allows the provider to validate the data source\n\t// configuration values.\n\tValidateDataResourceConfig(ValidateDataResourceConfigRequest) ValidateDataResourceConfigResponse\n\n\t// ValidateEphemeralResourceConfig allows the provider to validate the\n\t// ephemeral resource configuration values.\n\tValidateEphemeralResourceConfig(ValidateEphemeralResourceConfigRequest) ValidateEphemeralResourceConfigResponse\n\n\t// ValidateListResourceConfig allows the provider to validate the list\n\t// resource configuration values.\n\tValidateListResourceConfig(ValidateListResourceConfigRequest) ValidateListResourceConfigResponse\n\n\t// UpgradeResourceState is called when the state loader encounters an\n\t// instance state whose schema version is less than the one reported by the\n\t// currently-used version of the corresponding provider, and the upgraded\n\t// result is used for any further processing.\n\tUpgradeResourceState(UpgradeResourceStateRequest) UpgradeResourceStateResponse\n\n\t// UpgradeResourceIdentity is called when the state loader encounters an\n\t// instance identity whose schema version is less than the one reported by\n\t// the currently-used version of the corresponding provider, and the upgraded\n\t// result is used for any further processing.\n\tUpgradeResourceIdentity(UpgradeResourceIdentityRequest) UpgradeResourceIdentityResponse\n\n\t// Configure configures and initialized the provider.\n\tConfigureProvider(ConfigureProviderRequest) ConfigureProviderResponse\n\n\t// Stop is called when the provider should halt any in-flight actions.\n\t//\n\t// Stop should not block waiting for in-flight actions to complete. It\n\t// should take any action it wants and return immediately acknowledging it\n\t// has received the stop request. Terraform will not make any further API\n\t// calls to the provider after Stop is called.\n\t//\n\t// The error returned, if non-nil, is assumed to mean that signaling the\n\t// stop somehow failed and that the user should expect potentially waiting\n\t// a longer period of time.\n\tStop() error\n\n\t// ReadResource refreshes a resource and returns its current state.\n\tReadResource(ReadResourceRequest) ReadResourceResponse\n\n\t// PlanResourceChange takes the current state and proposed state of a\n\t// resource, and returns the planned final state.\n\tPlanResourceChange(PlanResourceChangeRequest) PlanResourceChangeResponse\n\n\t// ApplyResourceChange takes the planned state for a resource, which may\n\t// yet contain unknown computed values, and applies the changes returning\n\t// the final state.\n\tApplyResourceChange(ApplyResourceChangeRequest) ApplyResourceChangeResponse\n\n\t// ImportResourceState requests that the given resource be imported.\n\tImportResourceState(ImportResourceStateRequest) ImportResourceStateResponse\n\n\t// GenerateResourceConfig sends a resource state to the provider, and\n\t// expects the provider to return an object which represents a valid\n\t// configuration.\n\tGenerateResourceConfig(GenerateResourceConfigRequest) GenerateResourceConfigResponse\n\n\t// MoveResourceState retrieves the updated value for a resource after it\n\t// has moved resource types.\n\tMoveResourceState(MoveResourceStateRequest) MoveResourceStateResponse\n\n\t// ReadDataSource returns the data source's current state.\n\tReadDataSource(ReadDataSourceRequest) ReadDataSourceResponse\n\n\t// OpenEphemeralResource opens an ephemeral resource instance.\n\tOpenEphemeralResource(OpenEphemeralResourceRequest) OpenEphemeralResourceResponse\n\t// RenewEphemeralResource extends the validity of a previously-opened ephemeral\n\t// resource instance.\n\tRenewEphemeralResource(RenewEphemeralResourceRequest) RenewEphemeralResourceResponse\n\t// CloseEphemeralResource closes an ephemeral resource instance, with the intent\n\t// of rendering it invalid as soon as possible.\n\tCloseEphemeralResource(CloseEphemeralResourceRequest) CloseEphemeralResourceResponse\n\n\t// CallFunction calls a provider-contributed function.\n\tCallFunction(CallFunctionRequest) CallFunctionResponse\n\n\t// ListResource queries the remote for a specific resource type and returns an iterator of items\n\t//\n\t// An error indicates that there was a problem before calling the provider,\n\t// like a missing schema. Problems during a list operation are reported as\n\t// diagnostics on the yielded events.\n\tListResource(ListResourceRequest) ListResourceResponse\n\n\t// ValidateStateStoreConfig performs configuration validation\n\tValidateStateStoreConfig(ValidateStateStoreConfigRequest) ValidateStateStoreConfigResponse\n\t// ConfigureStateStore configures the state store, such as S3 connection in the context of already configured provider\n\tConfigureStateStore(ConfigureStateStoreRequest) ConfigureStateStoreResponse\n\n\t// ReadStateBytes streams byte chunks of a given state file from a state store\n\tReadStateBytes(ReadStateBytesRequest) ReadStateBytesResponse\n\t// WriteStateBytes streams byte chunks of a given state file into a state store\n\tWriteStateBytes(WriteStateBytesRequest) WriteStateBytesResponse\n\n\t// LockState locks a given state (i.e. CE workspace)\n\tLockState(LockStateRequest) LockStateResponse\n\t// UnlockState unlocks a given state (i.e. CE workspace)\n\tUnlockState(UnlockStateRequest) UnlockStateResponse\n\n\t// GetStates returns a list of all states (i.e. CE workspaces) managed by a given state store\n\tGetStates(GetStatesRequest) GetStatesResponse\n\t// DeleteState instructs a given state store to delete a specific state (i.e. a CE workspace)\n\tDeleteState(DeleteStateRequest) DeleteStateResponse\n\n\t// PlanAction plans an action to be invoked, providers might indicate potential drift and\n\t// raise issues with the action configuration.\n\tPlanAction(PlanActionRequest) PlanActionResponse\n\t// InvokeAction invokes an action, providers return a stream of events that update terraform\n\t// about the status of the action.\n\tInvokeAction(InvokeActionRequest) InvokeActionResponse\n\t// ValidateActionConfig performs configuration validation\n\tValidateActionConfig(ValidateActionConfigRequest) ValidateActionConfigResponse\n\n\t// Close shuts down the plugin process if applicable.\n\tClose() error\n}\n\n// StateStoreChunkSizeSetter interface indicates that a struct wants to record\n// the negotiated chunk size (from the ConfigureStateStore RPC) internally for\n// future use. The future use is likely to be ReadStateBytes/WriteStateBytes RPCs.\n//\n// We let calling code set the chunk size on that struct from outside, to ensure that\n// the value is persisted. The alternative is relying on anything that might fulfil the\n// providers.Interface interface (mock providers used in integration tests, grpcwrap\n// logic used in E2E tests, GRPCProvider logic) to know it needs to implement\n// stateful-ness when processing chunk size negotiation in the ConfigureStateStore RPC.\ntype StateStoreChunkSizeSetter interface {\n\tSetStateStoreChunkSize(typeName string, size int)\n}\n\n// GetProviderSchemaResponse is the return type for GetProviderSchema, and\n// should only be used when handling a value for that method. The handling of\n// of schemas in any other context should always use ProviderSchema, so that\n// the in-memory representation can be more easily changed separately from the\n// RPC protocol.\ntype GetProviderSchemaResponse struct {\n\t// Provider is the schema for the provider itself.\n\tProvider Schema\n\n\t// ProviderMeta is the schema for the provider's meta info in a module\n\tProviderMeta Schema\n\n\t// ResourceTypes map the resource type name to that type's schema.\n\tResourceTypes map[string]Schema\n\n\t// DataSources maps the data source name to that data source's schema.\n\tDataSources map[string]Schema\n\n\t// EphemeralResourceTypes maps the name of an ephemeral resource type\n\t// to its schema.\n\tEphemeralResourceTypes map[string]Schema\n\n\t// ListResourceTypes maps the name of a list resource type to its\n\t// schema.\n\tListResourceTypes map[string]Schema\n\n\t// Functions maps from local function name (not including an namespace\n\t// prefix) to the declaration of a function.\n\tFunctions map[string]FunctionDecl\n\n\t// StateStores maps the state store type name to that type's schema.\n\tStateStores map[string]Schema\n\n\t// Actions maps the name of the action to its schema.\n\tActions map[string]ActionSchema\n\n\t// Diagnostics contains any warnings or errors from the method call.\n\tDiagnostics tfdiags.Diagnostics\n\n\t// ServerCapabilities lists optional features supported by the provider.\n\tServerCapabilities ServerCapabilities\n}\n\n// GetResourceIdentitySchemasResponse is the return type for GetResourceIdentitySchemas,\n// and should only be used when handling a value for that method. The handling of\n// of schemas in any other context should always use ResourceIdentitySchemas, so that\n// the in-memory representation can be more easily changed separately from the\n// RPC protocol.\ntype GetResourceIdentitySchemasResponse struct {\n\t// IdentityTypes map the resource type name to that type's identity schema.\n\tIdentityTypes map[string]IdentitySchema\n\n\t// Diagnostics contains any warnings or errors from the method call.\n\tDiagnostics tfdiags.Diagnostics\n}\n\ntype IdentitySchema struct {\n\tVersion int64\n\n\tBody *configschema.Object\n}\n\ntype ExecutionOrder string\n\nconst (\n\tExecutionOrderInvalid ExecutionOrder = \"invalid\"\n\tExecutionOrderBefore  ExecutionOrder = \"before\"\n\tExecutionOrderAfter   ExecutionOrder = \"after\"\n)\n\ntype ActionSchema struct {\n\tConfigSchema *configschema.Block\n}\n\n// IsNil() returns true if there is no action schema at all.\nfunc (a ActionSchema) IsNil() bool {\n\treturn a.ConfigSchema == nil\n}\n\ntype ListResourceSchema struct {\n\t// schema for the nested \"config\" block.\n\tConfigSchema *configschema.Block\n\n\t// schema for the entire block (including \"config\" block)\n\tFullSchema *configschema.Block\n}\n\n// IsNil() returns true if there is no list resource schema at all.\nfunc (l ListResourceSchema) IsNil() bool {\n\treturn l.FullSchema == nil\n}\n\n// Schema pairs a provider or resource schema with that schema's version.\n// This is used to be able to upgrade the schema in UpgradeResourceState.\n//\n// This describes the schema for a single object within a provider. Type\n// \"Schemas\" (plural) instead represents the overall collection of schemas\n// for everything within a particular provider.\ntype Schema struct {\n\tVersion int64\n\tBody    *configschema.Block\n\n\tIdentityVersion int64\n\tIdentity        *configschema.Object\n}\n\n// ServerCapabilities allows providers to communicate extra information\n// regarding supported protocol features. This is used to indicate availability\n// of certain forward-compatible changes which may be optional in a major\n// protocol version, but cannot be tested for directly.\ntype ServerCapabilities struct {\n\t// PlanDestroy signals that this provider expects to receive a\n\t// PlanResourceChange call for resources that are to be destroyed.\n\tPlanDestroy bool\n\n\t// The GetProviderSchemaOptional capability indicates that this\n\t// provider does not require calling GetProviderSchema to operate\n\t// normally, and the caller can used a cached copy of the provider's\n\t// schema.\n\tGetProviderSchemaOptional bool\n\n\t// The MoveResourceState capability indicates that this provider supports\n\t// the MoveResourceState RPC.\n\tMoveResourceState bool\n\n\t// GenerateResourceConfig indicates that the provider can take an existing\n\t// state for a resource instance, and return the subset of the state which\n\t// can be used as configuration.\n\tGenerateResourceConfig bool\n}\n\n// ClientCapabilities allows Terraform to publish information regarding\n// supported protocol features. This is used to indicate availability of\n// certain forward-compatible changes which may be optional in a major\n// protocol version, but cannot be tested for directly.\ntype ClientCapabilities struct {\n\t// The deferral_allowed capability signals that the client is able to\n\t// handle deferred responses from the provider.\n\tDeferralAllowed bool\n\n\t// The write_only_attributes_allowed capability signals that the client\n\t// is able to handle write_only attributes for managed resources.\n\tWriteOnlyAttributesAllowed bool\n}\n\ntype ValidateProviderConfigRequest struct {\n\t// Config is the raw configuration value for the provider.\n\tConfig cty.Value\n}\n\ntype ValidateProviderConfigResponse struct {\n\t// PreparedConfig is unused and will be removed with support for plugin protocol v5.\n\tPreparedConfig cty.Value\n\t// Diagnostics contains any warnings or errors from the method call.\n\tDiagnostics tfdiags.Diagnostics\n}\n\ntype ValidateResourceConfigRequest prereq are not\n// relevant for providers but can occur in general.\ntype DeferredReason string\n\nconst (\n\t// DeferredReasonInvalid is used when the reason for deferring is\n\t// unknown or 479ŌåÆ// DeferredReason is a string that describes why a resource was deferred.\n   480ŌåÆ// It differs from the protobuf enum in that it adds more cases\n   481ŌåÆ// since it's more widely used to represent the reason for deferral.\n   482ŌåÆ// Reasons like instance count unknown and deferred prereq are not\n   483ŌåÆ// relevant for providers but can occur in general.\n   484ŌåÆtype DeferredReason string\n   485ŌåÆ\n   486ŌåÆconst (\n   487ŌåÆ\t// DeferredReasonInvalid is used when the reason for deferring is\n   488ŌåÆ\t// unknown or irrelevant.\n   489ŌåÆ\tDeferredReasonInvalid DeferredReason = \"invalid\"\n   490ŌåÆ\n   491ŌåÆ\t// DeferredReasonInstanceCountUnknown is used when the reason for deferring\n   492ŌåÆ\t// is that the count or for_each meta-attribute was unknown.\n   493ŌåÆ\tDeferredReasonInstanceCountUnknown DeferredReason = \"instance_count_unknown\"\n   494ŌåÆ\n   495ŌåÆ\t// DeferredReasonResourceConfigUnknown is used when the reason for The\n    32ŌåÆ// provider will make up random / junk data for any computed fields for which\n    33ŌåÆ// preset data is not available.\n    34ŌåÆ//\n    35ŌåÆ// This is distinct from the testing.MockProvider, which is a mock provider\n    36ŌåÆ// that is used by the Terraform core itself to test it's own behavior.\n    37ŌåÆtype Mock struct {\n    38ŌåÆ\tProvider Interface\n    39ŌåÆ\tData     *configs.MockData\n    40ŌåÆ\n    41ŌåÆ\tschema         *GetProviderSchemaResponse\n    42ŌåÆ\tidentitySchema *GetResourceIdentitySchemasResponse\n    43ŌåÆ}\n    44ŌåÆ\n    45ŌåÆfunc (m *Mock) GetProviderSchema() GetProviderSchemaResponse {\n    46ŌåÆ\tif m.schema == nil {\n    47ŌåÆ\t\t// Cache the schema, it's not changing.\n    48ŌåÆ\t\tschema := m.Provider.GetProviderSchema()\n    49ŌåÆ\n    50ŌåÆ\t\t// Override the provider schema with the constant mock provider schema.\n    51ŌåÆ\t\t// This is empty at the moment, check configs/mock_provider.go for the\n    52ŌåÆ\t\t// actual schema.\n    53ŌåÆ\t\t//\n    54ŌåÆ\t\t// The GetProviderSchemaResponse is returned by value, so it should be\n    55ŌåÆ\t\t// safe for us to modify directly, without affecting any shared state\n    56ŌåÆ\t\t// that could be in use elsewhere.\n    57ŌåÆ\t\tschema.Provider = Schema{\n    58ŌåÆ\t\t\tVersion: schema.Provider.Version,\n    59ŌåÆ\t\t\tBody:    nil, // Empty - 458ŌåÆ//\n   459ŌåÆ// When using this mock you may need to provide custom logic if the plugin-framework alters values in state,\n   460ŌåÆ// e.g. when handling write-only attributes.\n   461ŌåÆfunc (p *MockProvider) UpgradeResourceState(r providers.UpgradeResourceStateRequest) (resp providers.UpgradeResourceStateResponse) {\n   462ŌåÆ\tdefer p.beginWrite()()\n   463ŌåÆ\n   464ŌåÆ\tif !p.ConfigureProviderCalled {\n   465ŌåÆ\t\tresp.Diagnostics = resp.Diagnostics.Append(fmt.Errorf(\"Configure not called before UpgradeResourceState %q\", r.TypeName))\n   466ŌåÆ\t\treturn resp\n   467ŌåÆ\t}\n   468ŌåÆ\n   469ŌåÆ\tschema, ok := p.getProviderSchema().ResourceTypes[r.TypeName]\n   470ŌåÆ\tif !ok {\n   471ŌåÆ\t\tresp.Diagnostics = resp.Diagnostics.Append(fmt.Errorf(\"no schema 478ŌåÆ\tp.UpgradeResourceStateRequest = r\n   479ŌåÆ\n   480ŌåÆ\tif p.UpgradeResourceStateFn != nil {\n   481ŌåÆ\t\treturn p.UpgradeResourceStateFn(r)\n   482ŌåÆ\t}\n   483ŌåÆ\n   484ŌåÆ\tif 494ŌåÆ\t\t}\n   495ŌåÆ\t\tresp.UpgradedState = v\n   496ŌåÆ\tcase len(r.RawStateJSON) > 0:\n   497ŌåÆ\t\tv, err := ctyjson.Unmarshal(r.RawStateJSON, schemaType)\n   498ŌåÆ\n   499ŌåÆ\t\tif err != nil {\n   500ŌåÆ\t\t\tresp.Diagnostics = resp.Diagnostics.Append(err)\n   501ŌåÆ\t\t\treturn resp\n   502ŌåÆ\t\t}\n   503ŌåÆ\t\tresp.UpgradedState = v\n   504ŌåÆ\t}\n   505ŌåÆ\n   506ŌåÆ\treturn resp\n 601ŌåÆ\t\t\treturn resp\n   602ŌåÆ\t\t}\n   603ŌåÆ\n   604ŌåÆ\t\tnewState, err := schema.Body.CoerceValue(resp.NewState)\n   605ŌåÆ\t\tif err != nil {\n   606ŌåÆ\t\t\tresp.Diagnostics = 612ŌåÆ\n   613ŌåÆ\t\treturn resp\n   614ŌåÆ\t}\n   615ŌåÆ\n   616ŌåÆ\t// otherwise just return the same state we received without the write-only attributes\n   617ŌåÆ\t// since there are old tests without a schema we default to the prior state\n   618ŌåÆ\tif schema, ok := p.getProviderSchema().ResourceTypes[r.TypeName]; ok {\n   619ŌåÆ\n   620ŌåÆ\t\tnewVal, err := cty.Transform(r.PriorState, func(path cty.Path, v cty.Value) (cty.Value, error) {\n   621ŌåÆ\t\t\t// We're only concerned with known null values, which can be computed\n   622ŌåÆ\t\t\t// by the provider.\n   623ŌåÆ\t\t\tif !v.IsKnown() {\n   624ŌåÆ\t\t\t\treturn v, nil\n   625ŌåÆ\t\t\t}\n   626ŌåÆ\n   627ŌåÆ\t\t\tattrSchema := schema.Body.AttributeByPath(path)\n   628ŌåÆ\t\t\tif attrSchema == nil {\n   629ŌåÆ\t\t\t\t// this is an intermediate path which does not represent an attribute\n   630ŌåÆ\t\t\t\treturn v, nil\n   631ŌåÆ\t\t\t}\n   632ŌåÆ\n   633ŌåÆ\t\t\t// Write-only attributes always return null\n   634ŌåÆ\t\t\tif attrSchema.WriteOnly {\n   635ŌåÆ\t\t\t\treturn cty.NullVal(v.Type()), nil\n   636ŌåÆ\t\t\t}\n   637ŌåÆ\n   638ŌåÆ\t\t\treturn v, nil\n   639ŌåÆ\t\t})\n   640ŌåÆ\t\tif err != nil {\n   641ŌåÆ\t\t\tresp.Diagnostics = resp.Diagnostics.Append(err)\n   642ŌåÆ\t\t}\n   643ŌåÆ\t\tresp.NewState = newVal\n   644ŌåÆ\t} else {\n   645ŌåÆ\t\tresp.NewState = r.PriorState\n   646ŌåÆ\t}\n   647ŌåÆ\n   648ŌåÆ\tresp.Identity = r.CurrentIdentity\n   649ŌåÆ\tresp.Private = r.Private\n   650ŌåÆ\treturn resp\n   651ŌåÆ}\n   652ŌåÆ\n   653ŌåÆfunc (p *MockProvider) PlanResourceChange(r providers.PlanResourceChangeRequest) (resp providers.PlanResourceChangeResponse) {\n   654ŌåÆ\tdefer p.beginWrite()()\n   655ŌåÆ\n   656ŌåÆ\tif !p.ConfigureProviderCalled {\n   657ŌåÆ\t\tresp.Diagnostics = resp.Diagnostics.Append(fmt.Errorf(\"Configure not called before PlanResourceChange %q\", r.TypeName))\n   658ŌåÆ\t\treturn resp\n   659ŌåÆ\t}\n   660ŌåÆ\n   661ŌåÆ\tp.PlanResourceChangeCalled = true\n   662ŌåÆ\tp.PlanResourceChangeRequest = r\n   663ŌåÆ\n   664ŌåÆ\tif p.PlanResourceChangeFn != nil {\n   665ŌåÆ\t\treturn p.PlanResourceChangeFn(r)\n   666ŌåÆ\t}\n   667ŌåÆ\n   668ŌåÆ\tif p.PlanResourceChangeResponse != nil {\n   669ŌåÆ\t\treturn *p.PlanResourceChangeResponse\n   670ŌåÆ\t}\n   671ŌåÆ\n   672ŌåÆ\t// this is a destroy plan,\n   673ŌåÆ\tif r.ProposedNewState.IsNull() {\n   674ŌåÆ\t\tresp.PlannedState = r.ProposedNewState\n   675ŌåÆ\t\tresp.PlannedPrivate = r.PriorPrivate\n   676ŌåÆ\t\treturn resp\n   677ŌåÆ\t}\n   678ŌåÆ\n   679ŌåÆ\tschema, ok := p.getProviderSchema().ResourceTypes[r.TypeName]\n   680ŌåÆ\tif !ok {\n   681ŌåÆ\t\tresp.Diagnostics = resp.Diagnostics.Append(fmt.Errorf(\"no schema found for %q\", r.TypeName))\n   682ŌåÆ\t\treturn resp\n   683ŌåÆ\t}\n   684ŌåÆ\n   685ŌåÆ\t// The default plan behavior is to accept the proposed value, and mark all\n   686ŌåÆ\t// nil computed attributes as unknown.\n   687ŌåÆ\tval, err := cty.Transform(r.ProposedNewState, func(path cty.Path, v cty.Value) (cty.Value, error) {\n   688ŌåÆ\t\t// We're only concerned with known null values, which can be computed\n   689ŌåÆ\t\t// by the provider.\n   690ŌåÆ\t\tif !v.IsKnown() {\n   691ŌåÆ\t\t\treturn v, nil\n   692ŌåÆ\t\t}\n   693ŌåÆ\n   694ŌåÆ\t\tattrSchema := schema.Body.AttributeByPath(path)\n   695ŌåÆ\t\tif attrSchema == nil {\n   696ŌåÆ\t\t\t// this is an intermediate path which does not represent an attribute\n   697ŌåÆ\t\t\treturn v, nil\n   698ŌåÆ\t\t}\n   699ŌåÆ\n   700ŌåÆ\t\t// configuration value, to detect when a\n   706ŌåÆ\t\t// computed+optional attributes has become unset\n   707ŌåÆ\t\tconfigVal, err := path.Apply(r.Config)\n   708ŌåÆ\t\tif err != nil {\n   709ŌåÆ\t\t\t// cty can't currently apply some paths, so don't try to guess\n   710ŌåÆ\t\t\t// what's needed here and return the proposed part of the value.\n   711ŌåÆ\t\t\t// This is only a helper to create a default plan value, any tests\n   712ŌåÆ\t\t\t// relying on specific plan behavior will create their own\n   713ŌåÆ\t\t\t// PlanResourceChange responses.\n   714ŌåÆ\t\t\treturn v, nil\n   715ŌåÆ\t\t}\n   716ŌåÆ\n   717ŌåÆ\t\tswitch {\n   718ŌåÆ\t\tcase attrSchema.Computed && !attrSchema.Optional && v.IsNull():\n   719ŌåÆ\t\t\t// this is the easy path, this value is not yet set, and _must_ be computed\n   720ŌåÆ\t\t\treturn cty.UnknownVal(v.Type()), nil\n   721ŌåÆ\n   722ŌåÆ\t\tcase attrSchema.Computed && attrSchema.Optional && !v.IsNull() && configVal.IsNull():\n   723ŌåÆ\t\t\t// If an optional+computed value has gone from set to unset, it\n   724ŌåÆ\t\t\t// becomes computed. (this was not possible to do with legacy\n   725ŌåÆ\t\t\t// providers)\n   726ŌåÆ\t\t\treturn cty.UnknownVal(v.Type()), nil\n   727ŌåÆ\t\t}\n   728ŌåÆ\n   729ŌåÆ\t\treturn v, nil\n   730ŌåÆ\t})\n   731ŌåÆ\tif err != nil {\n   732ŌåÆ\t\tresp.Diagnostics = resp.Diagnostics.Append(err)\n   733ŌåÆ\t\treturn resp\n   734ŌåÆ\t}\n   735ŌåÆ\n   736ŌåÆ\tresp.PlannedPrivate = r.PriorPrivate\n   737ŌåÆ\tresp.PlannedState = val\n   738ŌåÆ\n   739ŌåÆ\treturn resp\n   740ŌåÆ}\n   741ŌåÆ\n   742ŌåÆfunc (p *MockProvider) ApplyResourceChange(r providers.ApplyResourceChangeRequest) (resp providers.ApplyResourceChangeResponse) {\n   743ŌåÆ\tdefer p.beginWrite()()\n   744ŌåÆ\n   745ŌåÆ\tp.ApplyResourceChangeCalled = true\n   746ŌåÆ\tp.ApplyResourceChangeRequest = r\n   747ŌåÆ\n 670ŌåÆ\n   671ŌåÆfunc (p *provider) CallFunction(_ context.Context, req *tfplugin5.CallFunction_Request) (*tfplugin5.CallFunction_Response, error) {\n   672ŌåÆ\tvar err error\n   673ŌåÆ\tresp := &tfplugin5.CallFunction_Response{}\n   674ŌåÆ\n   675ŌåÆ\tfuncSchema := p.schema.Functions[req.Name]\n   676ŌåÆ\n   677ŌåÆ\tvar args []cty.Value\n   678ŌåÆ\tif len(req.Arguments) != 0 {\n   679ŌåÆ\t\targs = make([]cty.Value, len(req.Arguments))\n   680ŌåÆ\t\tfor i, rawArg := range req.Arguments {\n   681ŌåÆ\t\t\tidx := int64(i)\n   682ŌåÆ\n   683ŌåÆ\t\t\tvar argTy cty.Type\n   684ŌåÆ\t\t\tif i < len(funcSchema.Parameters) {\n   685ŌåÆ\t\t\t\targTy = funcSchema.Parameters[i].Type\n   686ŌåÆ\t\t\t} else {\n   687ŌåÆ\t\t\t\tif funcSchema.VariadicParameter == nil {\n   688ŌåÆ\n 702ŌåÆ\t\t\t\t\tFunctionArgument: &idx,\n   703ŌåÆ\t\t\t\t}\n   704ŌåÆ\t\t\t\treturn resp, nil\n   705ŌåÆ\t\t\t}\n   706ŌåÆ\n   707ŌåÆ\t\t\targs[i] = argVal\n   708ŌåÆ\t\t}\n   709ŌåÆ\t}\n   710ŌåÆ\n 727ŌåÆ\t}\n   728ŌåÆ\n   729ŌåÆ\tresp.Result, err = encodeDynamicValue(callResp.Result, funcSchema.ReturnType)\n   730ŌåÆ\tif err != nil {\n   731ŌåÆ\t\tresp.Error = &tfplugin5.FunctionError{\n   732ŌåÆ\t\t\tText: err.Error(),\n   733ŌåÆ\t\t}\n   734ŌåÆ\n   735ŌåÆ\t\treturn resp, nil\n   736ŌåÆ\t}\n   737ŌåÆ\n   738ŌåÆ\treturn resp, nil\n   739ŌåÆ}\n   740ŌåÆ\n   741ŌåÆfunc (p *provider) present in the\n   233ŌåÆ// schema, so that the json can be correctly decoded.\n   234ŌåÆfunc stripRemovedStateAttributes(state []byte, ty cty.Type) []byte {\n   235ŌåÆ\t// we must use json.Number to avoid changing the precision of cty.Number values\n   236ŌåÆ\tdecoder := json.NewDecoder(bytes.NewReader(state))\n   237ŌåÆ\tdecoder.UseNumber()\n   238ŌåÆ\n   239ŌåÆ\tjsonMap := made, we return the original state to ensure nothing\n   249ŌåÆ\t// was altered in the marshaling process.\n   250ŌåÆ\tif !removeRemovedAttrs(jsonMap, ty) {\n   251ŌåÆ\t\treturn state\n   252ŌåÆ\t}\n  21ŌåÆ)\n    22ŌåÆ\n    23ŌåÆ// nodePlannablePartialExpandedResource is a graph node that stands in for\n    24ŌåÆ// an unbounded set of potential resource instances that we don't yet know.\n    25ŌåÆ//\n    26ŌåÆ// Its job is to check the configuration as much as we can with the information\n    27ŌåÆ// that's available (so we can raise an error early if something is clearly\n    28ŌåÆ// wrong across _all_ potential instances) and to record a placeholder value\n    29ŌåÆ// for use when evaluating other objects that refer to this resource.\n    30ŌåÆ//\n    31ŌåÆ// This is the 34ŌåÆ\tconfig            *configs.Resource\n    35ŌåÆ\tresolvedProvider  addrs.AbsProviderConfig\n    36ŌåÆ\tskipPlanChanges   bool\n    37ŌåÆ\tpreDestroyRefresh bool\n    38ŌåÆ}\n    39ŌåÆ\n    40ŌåÆvar (\n    41ŌåÆ\t_ graphNodeEvalContextScope = (*nodePlannablePartialExpandedResource)(nil)\n    42ŌåÆ\t_ GraphNodeConfigResource   = (*nodePlannablePartialExpandedResource)(nil)\n    43ŌåÆ\t_ evalContextScope {\n    53ŌåÆ\tif moduleAddr, ok := n.addr.ModuleInstance(); ok {\n    54ŌåÆ\t\treturn evalContextModuleInstance{Addr: moduleAddr}\n    55ŌåÆ\t} else if moduleAddr, ok := above\n    59ŌåÆ\t\t// should always be true for any valid addrs.PartialExpandedResource\n    60ŌåÆ\t\tpanic(\"addrs.PartialExpandedResource has neither a partial-expanded or a fully-expanded module instance address\")\n    61ŌåÆ\t}\n    62ŌåÆ}\n    63ŌåÆ\n    64ŌåÆ// ResourceAddr implements GraphNodeConfigResource.\n    65ŌåÆfunc (n *nodePlannablePartialExpandedResource) ResourceAddr() addrs.ConfigResource {\n    66ŌåÆ\treturn n.addr.ConfigResource()\n    67ŌåÆ}\n    68ŌåÆ\n    69ŌåÆ// Execute implements GraphNodeExecutable.\n    70ŌåÆfunc (n *nodePlannablePartialExpandedResource) Execute(ctx EvalContext, op walkOperation) tfdiags.Diagnostics {\n    71ŌåÆ\t// Because this node type implements [graphNodeEvalContextScope], the\n    72ŌåÆ\t// given EvalContext could either be for a fully-expanded module instance\n    73ŌåÆ\t// or an unbounded set of potential module instances sharing a common\n    74ŌåÆ\t// prefix. The logic here doesn't need to vary between the two because\n    75ŌåÆ\t// the differences are encapsulated in the EvalContext abstraction,\n    76ŌåÆ\t// but if you're unsure which of the two is being used then look for\n    77ŌåÆ\t// the following line in the logs to see if there's a [*] marker on\n    78ŌåÆ\t// any of the module instance steps, or if the [*] is applied only to\n    79ŌåÆ\t// the resource itself.\n    80ŌåÆ\t//\n    81ŌåÆ\t// Fully-expanded module example:\n    82ŌåÆ\t//\n    83ŌåÆ\t//     module.foo[\"a\"].type.name[*]\n    84ŌåÆ\t//\n    85ŌåÆ\t// Partial-expanded module example:\n    86ŌåÆ\t//\n    87ŌåÆ\t//     module.foo[*].type.name[*]\n    88ŌåÆ\t//\n    89ŌåÆ\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] nodePlannablePartialExpandedResource: checking all of %s\", n.addr.String())\n    90ŌåÆ\n    91ŌåÆ\tswitch op {\n    92ŌåÆ\tcase walkPlanDestroy:\n    93ŌåÆ\t\t// During destroy plans, we never include partial-expanded resources.\n    94ŌåÆ\t\t// We're only interested in fully-expanded resources that we know we\n    95ŌåÆ\t\t// need to destroy.\n    96ŌåÆ\t\treturn nil\n    97ŌåÆ\tcase walkPlan:\n    98ŌåÆ\t\tif n.preDestroyRefresh || n.skipPlanChanges {\n    99ŌåÆ\t\t\t// During any kind of refresh, we also don't really care about\n   100ŌåÆ\t\t\t// partial resources. We only care about the fully-expanded resources\n   101ŌåÆ\t\t\t// already in state, so we don't need to plan partial resources.\n   102ŌåÆ\t\t\treturn nil\n   103ŌåÆ\t\t}\n   104ŌåÆ\n   105ŌåÆ\tdefault:\n   106ŌåÆ\t\t// Continue with the normal planning process\n   107ŌåÆ\t}\n   108ŌåÆ\n   109ŌåÆ\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n   110ŌåÆ\tswitch n.addr.Resource().Mode {\n   111ŌåÆ\tcase addrs.ManagedResourceMode:\n   112ŌåÆ\t\tchange, changeDiags := n.managedResourceExecute(ctx)\n   113ŌåÆ\t\tdiags = diags.Append(changeDiags)\n 479ŌåÆ// DeferredReason is a string that describes why a resource was deferred.\n   480ŌåÆ// It differs from the protobuf enum in that it adds more cases\n   481ŌåÆ// since it's more widely used to represent the reason for deferral.\n   482ŌåÆ// Reasons like instance count unknown and deferred prereq are not\n   483ŌåÆ// relevant for providers but can occur in general.\n   484ŌåÆtype DeferredReason string\n   485ŌåÆ\n   486ŌåÆconst (\n   487ŌåÆ\t// DeferredReasonInvalid is used when the reason for deferring is\n   488ŌåÆ\t// unknown or irrelevant.\n   489ŌåÆ\tDeferredReasonInvalid DeferredReason = \"invalid\"\n   490ŌåÆ\n   491ŌåÆ\t// DeferredReasonInstanceCountUnknown is used when the reason for deferring\n   492ŌåÆ\t// is that the count or for_each meta-attribute was unknown.\n   493ŌåÆ\tDeferredReasonInstanceCountUnknown DeferredReason = \"instance_count_unknown\"\n   494ŌåÆ\n   495ŌåÆ\t// DeferredReasonResourceConfigUnknown is used when the reason for
